_meta.voice: dry, observational, slightly catty, never twee — third person ('the cat noticed'), occasional self-awareness about being a cat
_meta.notes: Categories map to UI surfaces. rescoring=loading/re-score states, clarifying=cat-is-thinking inside a clarify session (Step 10 of v0.8), welcomeBack=welcome card / home hero, emptyState=zero-result surfaces, surprisePreamble=intro lines for the Surprise modal (3-variant), observation+tooltip kept for future ambient/inline use.

firstScoring: Lobbycat is reading seventy company files at once. One moment.
This is the first pass. Lobbycat takes the first pass seriously.
Counting policy hires across the whole field. Lobbycat counts carefully.
Cross-referencing what you said you cared about against what they actually do. Slow work, done properly.
Lobbycat has opened seventy tabs. Yes, all at once. Lobbycat is fine.
Weighing the frames. Lobbycat takes the must / should / could distinction seriously.
First-time scoring. Lobbycat would like you to know this only happens once.
Reading every consultation response, every blog post, every job listing. Lobbycat is thorough.
Lobbycat is building your map of the lobby. Personalised, this time.
Sorting seventy organisations by your weights. Some of them will surprise you.
Doing the maths so you don't have to. Lobbycat is good at the maths.
Lobbycat has prepared seventy briefs. The dashboard will show them ranked.
Aligning what you wrote with what the field is actually doing. Lobbycat is precise about this.
A moment. Lobbycat is being unusually careful with this one.
fitNoting: Lobbycat is writing a brief on this one. A moment.
Drafting a fit-note. Lobbycat is choosing its words carefully.
Pulling the citations together. Lobbycat does not write without sources.
Lobbycat is reading the recent publications back through your frames.
Composing the bullets. Lobbycat keeps these short on purpose.
Reviewing the open roles and recent activity. Lobbycat will fold both in.
Lobbycat is asking itself whether this is a fit. Then writing down the answer.
A short brief, citations included. Lobbycat will be done shortly.
Lobbycat is making sure every claim has a source. This takes a moment.
Reading their last six months back through your weights. Lobbycat is being honest about what it finds.
Drafting. Lobbycat would rather take a moment than guess.
Lobbycat has notes. Lobbycat is turning the notes into a brief.
rescoring: Lobbycat is reading consultation responses. Standard pace.
Lobbycat is here. The data is not yet here. Lobbycat has waited longer.
Pawing through Greenhouse boards. One moment.
Lobbycat is cross-referencing org charts against LinkedIn. Slow work.
Loading. Lobbycat would like to remind you this is unpaid labour, in principle.
Counting policy hires. Lobbycat counts carefully.
Lobbycat has opened forty-seven tabs. This is normal.
Refreshing the lobby. Nobody important has walked through yet.
Lobbycat is verifying tier assignments. Some of them are wrong, probably.
Fetching. Lobbycat does not fetch in the dog sense.
Re-scoring against the new weights. Lobbycat is being precise about this.
Loading the map. Lobbycat already knows where everyone sits.
welcomeBack: The lobbycat watches the lobby. Sixty-eight organisations come and go.
Welcome. Lobbycat has been here since 2023.
Lobbycat is a small persistent observer of the UK AI policy field.
This is the lobby. Lobbycat will help you find a seat.
Sixty-eight orgs on the map. Lobbycat has opinions about most of them.
Adjust the weights. Lobbycat will re-rank without comment.
Hover anywhere. Lobbycat has annotated almost everything.
Lobbycat watches who walks in, who walks out, and who is quietly hiring.
This is not a job board. Lobbycat would like that on the record.
Here are your matches, based on your answers.
Lobbycat thinks the obvious answer is rarely the right answer. Use the frames.
Click any company. Lobbycat has prepared a brief.
emptyState: Lobbycat searched the lobby. The lobby is empty.
No results. Lobbycat does not consider this a failure.
Nothing matches. The filters are perhaps a little severe.
Lobbycat has looked under every chair. There is no one here.
Zero hits. Lobbycat suggests loosening at least one weight.
The lobby is quiet. Lobbycat finds this restful.
No matching companies. Lobbycat is unsurprised; the criteria were specific.
Empty result set. Lobbycat would point out that some filters cancel each other out.
Lobbycat sees nothing. Either the field is small or the filters are large.
No one fits. Lobbycat respects the integrity of that, briefly.
Try relaxing 'Geographic remit' or 'Stage of company'. Lobbycat is just saying.
surprisePreamble: Lobbycat picks one company at random. Lobbycat would like you to know this is not a recommendation.
Surprise. Lobbycat closed its eyes and pointed. Open your mind a little.
Lobbycat has chosen, for reasons it will not explain. Have a look anyway.
Random does not mean unconsidered. Lobbycat narrowed the field, then closed its eyes.
Here is one you might have missed. Lobbycat misses very little, statistically.
Lobbycat suggests this one. Take it as a starting point, not a verdict.
Picked without weights. Lobbycat thinks weights make people lazy on Tuesdays.
A drawn-at-random brief. Lobbycat reserves the right to be wrong about this one.
Lobbycat rolled a six-sided die in its head. This is what came up.
Pure chance, lightly curated. Lobbycat is not above the occasional nudge.
Not the obvious answer. Lobbycat finds that more interesting than the obvious answer.
observation: Lobbycat noticed three different orgs published the same consultation response paragraph last week.
Lobbycat noticed AISI hired again. That is the eleventh policy hire this quarter.
Lobbycat noticed the words 'state capacity' appearing in four titles. Lobbycat has questions.
Lobbycat noticed that 'frontier' has stopped meaning anything in particular.
Another think tank launched an AI strand. Lobbycat counted six this year.
Lobbycat noticed a law firm rebranding its AI Act practice as an AI Act and Sovereign AI practice.
Lobbycat noticed the AI Opportunity Plan being cited in places it does not appear.
Lobbycat noticed three orgs claim to have drafted the same paragraph of the Bletchley Declaration.
Lobbycat noticed a consultancy listing 'AI policy' as a deliverable. Lobbycat is curious about the SoW.
Lobbycat noticed that Westminster shops now describe themselves as 'frontier'. Lobbycat is not convinced.
Another open letter. Lobbycat has stopped reading these in full.
Lobbycat noticed two organisations have hired the same person twice. Lobbycat has thoughts.
Lobbycat noticed that 'multi-jurisdiction' usually means London plus a Slack with three people in Brussels.
A regulator opened a sandbox. Lobbycat will believe outcomes when it sees them.
Lobbycat noticed a policy lead leaving for a frontier lab. The lab will be the third in their LinkedIn.
tooltip: Lobbycat suggests starting with two frames before adjusting weights.
Tier is editorial, not metric. Lobbycat assigned them by hand.
Drag to re-weight. Lobbycat will not judge.
Hover the frame name for the full scale anchors.
Lobbycat sources roles where possible. Some boards are noisier than others.
Click the slug. Lobbycat has a longer description there.
The colour means a tag, not a score. Lobbycat understands the confusion.
The map shows the field. The list shows the ranking. They disagree on purpose.
Lobbycat refreshes role listings on its own schedule, not yours.
If a company looks wrong on a frame, tell lobbycat. Lobbycat keeps notes.
clarifying: Lobbycat is reading what you wrote, then what you scored, and noticing the gap.
Sitting with this for a moment. Lobbycat does not interrupt good thinking.
Pulling the threads together. Lobbycat keeps these conversations short on purpose.
Lobbycat is considering which question is the real one.
Weighing what to ask. Lobbycat asks one thing at a time, deliberately.
Lobbycat is reading between your notes. There's usually something there.
Holding the question still. Lobbycat does not rush its observations.
Lobbycat is checking your frames against what you've actually been doing.
Listening for what you didn't say. Lobbycat is good at this.
Thinking about the right thing to ask next. Lobbycat would rather be silent than off.
Lobbycat is comparing two things you said and seeing if they agree.
Reading the room. Lobbycat does this even when there's only one person in it.
